danger_signals.visual_form: Sudden disappearance of species from map display; population collapse visualization
danger_signals.meaning: Ecosystem imbalance or catastrophic world-state change; player intervention may be needed

danger_signals.visual_form: Atmospheric dust layer increase; temperature drop indicator
danger_signals.meaning: Natural disaster reducing planetary temperature and threatening life stability

danger_signals.visual_form: Civilization collapse; radiation markers; nuclear winter effect (atmospheric darkening)
danger_signals.meaning: Advanced civilization self-destruction via technology dependence; cascading ecosystem failure

danger_signals.visual_form: Water body depletion on map; barren land expansion
danger_signals.meaning: Extreme global warming; planetary habitability crisis

danger_signals.visual_form: Water body interaction with seismic activity markers
danger_signals.meaning: Earthquake in ocean triggering secondary catastrophe

danger_signals.visual_form: Population-dependent disaster indicator; civilization health decline
danger_signals.meaning: Population density and sanitation state correlate with biological threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Atmospheric quality degradation display; environmental stress indicators
danger_signals.meaning: Industrial civilization impact exceeding planetary regeneration capacity

danger_signals.visual_form: Polar ice visual reduction; sea level rise on map
danger_signals.meaning: Global warming threshold crossed; cascading hydrological change

safety_signals.visual_form: Stable gas percentage readings (within optimal range)
safety_signals.meaning: Planet supports multicellular animal life; ecosystem stability

safety_signals.visual_form: Temperature display within habitable range; moderate climate visualization
safety_signals.meaning: Conditions support advanced life development and civilization

safety_signals.visual_form: Multiple species present on map; population growth indicators
Population growth; technological progression through visible age stages
safety_signals.meaning: Species reached sentience and organized society without collapse
Player capacity to intervene; planetary development momentum maintained

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Emergent from destroyed nanotech-age cities after nuclear explosion; treats organic life as separate entity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears in Nanotech Age civilization zones; replaces organic life when triggered

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Species-specific visual markers (radiates, molluscs, cetaceans, fishes, birds, etc.)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Self-replicating robots treated as existential threat in Earth 2XXX scenario
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Cities and settlements appear on habitable landmasses; technology age visible in infrastructure complexity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Single-celled organism markers; minimal landmass complexity
Taxa appearance on map (radiates, fishes, birds, molluscs, cetaceans); increased biodiversity indicators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Species thrive or die out based on environmental parameters; natural selection visualization

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sapient species designation; civilization formation begins
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Species transition to organized society; cultural/technological markers appear

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: City infrastructure; pollution indicators increase; energy consumption metrics rise
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Civilization impacts planetary systems; potential for self-destruction through nuclear war

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Highest technology level cities; advanced infrastructure density
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Potential trigger for machine life emergence if destroyed; represents peak technological civilization

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Species leaves planet visualization; civilization transcends planetary bounds
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Win condition achieved; sentient life achieves interstellar capability

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sun becomes red giant; all life eliminated from map
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game ends; inevitable heat death visualized

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Barren/hostile world transforms into habitable world; climate indicators normalize
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player-guided planetary engineering; life introduction and evolution acceleration

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Self-replicating robots overlay; nuclear warfare damage markers; civilization collapse indicators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must prevent self-replication and nuclear destruction; optional great flood intervention

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Daisy color change relative to temperature; single biome landscape
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Self-regulating ecosystem; demonstrates Daisyworld model feedback systems

environmental_storytelling.example: Percentages shown to three decimal places reveal player impact on oxygen, CO2, and other gases; visible correlation between composition and life support
Landmass arrangement changes over deep time; player can adjust drift rate to influence species distribution and climate patterns
Volcanic eruptions create dust layer (visual darkening); earthquakes trigger tsunami waves; consequences persist and compound
Different taxa (molluscs, cetaceans, radiates) occupy distinct environmental niches; visual placement reveals ecological role and habitability requirements
City appearance evolves from primitive settlements through industrial to nanotech; visual complexity increases with civilization development
2001: A Space Odyssey reference; visible tool that increases lifeform intelligence through extraterrestrial influence; shows game's metaphorical approach to guided evolution
Omega (Ω) unit display limits player action; cost feedback (50 units per terrain square, 500 for terraforming device) reinforces resource scarcity narrative

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Population growth; expansion across suitable habitats; evolutionary mutation progression
Population decline; range contraction; potential extinction if stress persists
Sudden disappearance from map; reset of evolutionary potential; ecological niche vacancy created
Slow settlement expansion; minimal resource extraction; low pollution
Rapid city growth; pollution accumulation; energy consumption spike; vulnerability to nuclear war
Peak technological infrastructure; potential for machine life emergence if catastrophically destroyed
Nuclear winter visualization; population crash; potential machine life emergence
Treats organic life as distinct entity; self-replicating threat in Earth 2XXX scenario; represents technology-gone-wrong narrative
Parameters remain within habitable ranges; Gaia hypothesis self-regulation demonstrated
Temperature rises; ice caps melt; oceans boil away; visual progression toward sterile planet
Daisies adjust color based on temperature; ecosystem self-regulates; demonstrates feedback systems

notes: SimEarth communicates world-state primarily through quantitative parameter displays (atmospheric composition, temperature, energy budget) coupled with qualitative visual representations (biodiversity presence, city infrastructure complexity, disaster effects). The game uses no traditional faction conflict system; instead, political/civilization danger emerges from self-inflicted technological risk (nuclear war) or ecosystem collapse. Environmental storytelling relies heavily on long-term consequence visualization—volcanic dust persists visually, species extinctions create permanent landscape changes, pollution compounds over time. The game's core design philosophy treats the planet as a self-regulating living system (Gaia hypothesis), so safety signals are inherently about maintaining balance rather than winning against antagonists. Danger signals communicate when that balance is breaking. The Nanotech Age scenario endpoint where machine life can emerge is the only true 'faction' conflict, treating it as an evolutionary divergence threat rather than traditional warfare.
